description_long: This is a compact two-story house with a dark, steeply pitched gable roof and a smaller overhanging roof above the ground floor. The exterior walls are finished in a light gray or off-white color, contrasted by dark trim around the windows and roof edges. The upper story features two large, square windows with a grid pattern, while the lower story has a single, smaller window with similar styling. The house has a symmetrical facade, with the main entryway likely located beneath the overhanging roof section. The overall design is simple and functional, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on geometric forms. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from masonry or concrete, with wooden or metal window frames and a tiled or metal roof.
description: A compact, two-story house with a steep dark gable roof, light walls, and symmetrical grid-patterned windows. The design is simple, with a prominent overhanging roof above the entry area.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large upper windows and a smaller lower window beneath a prominent overhanging roof.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the steep gable roof and the symmetrical window arrangement on the facade.
description_view_2: Rear or opposite side view, similar to the front but with less visible detail on the lower story.
description_view_3: Angled view from the other side, again emphasizing the gable roof and window symmetry.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed entry beneath an overhanging roof, likely with a simple door and minimal decorative elements.
